FLOW.COMMENT: splitIF - system service for splitting an inbound document into equivalent child documents

SEQUENCE.COMMENT: splitIF - system service for splitting an inbound document into equivalent child documents - splits content1
This service will split a grouped document (stored as content1) into a series of individual documents based on the 2 delimeters
specified in DelimBEG and DelimEND. These 2 delimeters are specified as parameters to this service. The output of this service is such
that it will create and start a child transaction for each separate document that it finds. If multiple documents (more than one) are found,
then this service will force the workflow to end, since this will then be considered the 'parent' transaction, meaning no mapping or delivery
will occur. If only a single document is found, then this service will exit normally and allow the workflow to continue, under the assumption
that this is a child transaction and that mapping and delivery must occur on this single document.
Note that this service will properly recognize and handle a group header and footer. Both the group header and footer will be applied
to each child document. See the first DEBUG step for an example of how this looks.
It is assumed, of course, that the original collection contains documents that are all identical in type and format. This service is NOT suitable
Note that this service will NOT work on JSON or XML formatted content - see instead the splitIFjson and splitEDSR services
modified 1/31/23 to support OPT_SPLIT_CONTENT=contentx where content1 is the default
modified 7/17/25 to support OPT_HEADERFOOTER=false for some types of flat files that have record markers at the start of each record

MAP.COMMENT: DEBUG ONLY TEST4 - this process will not work on json documents

INVOKE.COMMENT: split the content by the 2 delimeters into docList, numDocs

BRANCH.COMMENT: depending on whether we have 1 doc or many docs, we fall through and do nothing, or split them and create children

SEQUENCE.NAME: numDocs>1
SEQUENCE.COMMENT: we have more than one document, so we have to fix up all documents with a new header and footer

INVOKE.COMMENT: split doc0 returning the header and a true 0 doc
split docN returning the true N doc and the footer

MAP.COMMENT: map doc0 and docN back into docList - also blankIfNull the footer
set header and footer to blank

SEQUENCE.NAME: CHILD
SEQUENCE.COMMENT: need to capture any errors in creating/starting the CHILD transaction, so that we can continue

record.value: 0 or the sessionid of the caller
if parent is not 0, this is the child number starting with 1

INVOKE.COMMENT: move the current workflow (the parent) to its last step, so that the parent can end quietly

SEQUENCE.NAME: $default
SEQUENCE.COMMENT: we have only a single document, so do nothing

INVOKE.COMMENT: content did not split - transaction will continue

Values.value: content did not split - transaction will continue
